stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Archaeologist Renée Souza and colleagues argue that early Polynesian settlers reached remote islands by following stellar navigation routes.
  • They propose that early voyagers used a network of stars, prevailing winds, and ocean currents that linked Tonga and Samoa to distant island chains.
  • Many of the wayfinding stations along these routes were later lost as oral traditions were disrupted.
  • The researchers describe these routes as “star paths threaded across the open Pacific.”
stem: The student wants to summarize the star-path hypothesis. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Early Polynesians may have reached remote islands by following stellar navigation routes that wove together stars, winds, and currents.
One argument about how Polynesians reached remote islands is the star-path hypothesis proposed by Renée Souza and colleagues.
The wayfinding routes likely combined “stars, winds, and currents,” according to Souza and colleagues.
As oral traditions were disrupted, many wayfinding stations were lost over time.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer because it captures the essence of the hypothesis—stellar navigation tying together stars, winds, and currents to reach remote islands.

Choice B identifies the hypothesis but doesn't summarize it. Choice C quotes a phrase without explaining the hypothesis. Choice D focuses on a secondary point.
